sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Dano Finance requests treasury funding to accelerate the DeFi Kernel as an open standard and deliver a registry, two DeFi Kernel-compatible primitives, and a Composable DeFi Transaction Builder SDK.
Recipient: Dano Finance, with Minswap Labs serving as budget administrator

Deliverables: DeFi Kernel registry website and submission process, Spot Leverage Order Book, American Options Market-Making protocol, Composable DeFi Transaction Builder SDK, Public documentation, reporting, KPI tracking, and security review or audit before mainnet releases
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 3,333,000 ADA, consisting of 3,300,000 ADA for delivery and 33,000 ADA for a 1% budget administration fee.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The delivery budget is allocated as 300,000 ADA for the registry and 1,000,000 ADA each for the Spot Leverage Order Book, American Options Protocol, and SDK.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Dano Finance says Cardano DeFi is fragmented across liquidity, metadata, and execution paths, which slows integrations and limits advanced financial applications.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The DeFi Kernel registry is intended to publish script hashes, datum and redeemer schemas, integration instructions, supported libraries, audit or security status, TVL or usage references, and discovery mechanisms for compatible contracts.
Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Dano Finance claims it currently has approximately USD 18M in TVL and more than 10,000 on-chain transactions.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The Spot Leverage Order Book work package will deliver a Cardano-native leveraged spot trading protocol with collateral, borrowing, debt accounting, position state, repayment, liquidation conditions, and settlement.
missing: Independent evidence for: Dano Finance says Cardano DeFi is fragmented across liquidity, metadata, and execution paths, which slows integrations and limits advanced financial applications.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 3,333,000 ADA, consisting of 3,300,000 ADA for delivery and 33,000 ADA for a 1% budget administration fee." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Dano Finance says Cardano DeFi is fragmented across liquidity, metadata, and execution paths, which slows integrations and limits advanced financial applications." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
